description:
Stop registering any-key specs for fns whose every reachable callsite — direct or indirect — has typed coverage.

## What

After .29.12.1 / .3 / .4 / .5, every cont, closure, spawn, and receive dispatch resolves to a narrow SpecId. The any-key fallback becomes dead weight for any fn whose call graph is fully typed.

- In `src/ir_typer.rs` (the fixed-point loop), skip registering an any-key for fn F when F's `callsite_keys` set is non-empty AND covers F's reachable shapes.
- Define "reachable shapes" precisely: union of (a) direct callsite keys, (b) cont-Cont keys with the right slot-0 source, (c) closure MakeClosure-resolved keys.
- An "entry-point-like" fn (no caller — e.g., `main`, top-level driver) still needs an any-key.

## Why

This is the .29.10 payoff. The transitive any-key cascade that blocked .29.10 disappears because indirect targets no longer need any-keys to cover the all-any case.

## Tests (TDD)

- The map_l/k_3 case from .29.10's investigation: assert `map_l` no longer has an any-key in `ModuleTypes.specs`.
- `main` still has an any-key.
- Full test suite + parity tests stay green.

## Out of scope

- Cross-module fns whose call graph isn't visible (defer to a separate cross-module concern).
title: Drop unnecessary any-key registrations
